name: MB1355C / NUCLEO-WB55RG board onboarding proof
description: Board onboarding draft for MB1355C / NUCLEO-WB55RG board onboarding proof.

inputs.requirements: Need deterministic board onboarding proof for STM32WB55RG app core, LED mapping, user button mapping, UART debug path, and simple BLE-oriented demo selection using official ST example references. Prefer board-level artifacts that can be turned into chip/system/example manifests.

summary: This board_onboarding_draft artifact provides deterministic proof for MB1355C/NUCLEO-WB55RG onboarding, verifying STM32WB55RG app core, LED/button mappings, UART debug path, and simple BLE demo via official ST examples, for conversion to chip/system manifests. It intentionally does not guarantee full production validation, custom HW tweaks, or comprehensive testing beyond referenced demos.

files.description: Board system manifest starter wired to console and board IO placeholders.
files.content:
name: "mb1355c"
chip: "../chips/stm32wb55.yaml"

connectors:
  - type: "uart"
    peripheral: "uart_console"
    endpoint: "host_console"

# TODO: replace placeholder pin mappings from board schematic.
board:
  leds:
    - id: "led_user"
      gpio: "TODO"
      active: "TODO"
  buttons:
    - id: "button_user"
      gpio: "TODO"
      active: "TODO"

# mb1355c

This example was synthesized by Foundry as a board onboarding starter.

## Scope

- App-core boot path
- RCC/GPIO/UART baseline
- Board LED and user button mapping
- BLE example selection reference only

## Recommended ST Example

- STM32CubeWB BLE_p2pServer
- STM32CubeWB BLE_LLD_Pressbutton

## Required Source Confirmation

- Confirm MCU part number and package
- Confirm VCP UART instance on ST-LINK
- Confirm LED and button GPIO mappings from schematic
- Confirm whether BLE scope is documentation-only or full simulator behavior

name: "mb1355c"
chip: ../../configs/chips/stm32wb55.yaml

connectors:
  - type: "uart"
    peripheral: "uart_console"
    endpoint: "host_console"

# Required Docs

- MCU reference manual
- MCU datasheet
- Board user manual / schematic
- Official STM32CubeWB example project used as proof workload

# External Components

- ST-LINK virtual COM port connection
- On-board user LEDs
- On-board user button
- BLE/radio subsystem is deferred unless engine support is explicitly added

# Validation

Run from `core/` after replacing placeholders:

```bash
cargo run -q -p labwired-cli -- \
  --firmware path/to/firmware.elf \
  --system examples/mb1355c/system.yaml \
  --max-steps 64
./scripts/unsupported_instruction_audit.sh \
  --firmware path/to/firmware.elf \
  --system configs/systems/mb1355c.yaml \
  --max-steps 200000 \
  --out-dir out/unsupported-audit/mb1355c
```

Expected evidence:

- PC/SP initialize correctly
- UART smoke output is deterministic
- LED/button paths are mapped and exercised

recommended_examples.name: STM32CubeWB BLE_p2pServer
recommended_examples.reason: Good proof target for NUCLEO-WB55RG board IO plus BLE-facing application flow.

recommended_examples.name: STM32CubeWB BLE_LLD_Pressbutton
recommended_examples.reason: Smaller board-level LED/button proof when radio fidelity is not the first milestone.

source_requirements: datasheet with memory map and package details
board user manual / schematic for LED and button mapping
official vendor firmware example for proof workload
validation_plan: Boot firmware on app core and verify PC/SP initialization.
Confirm deterministic UART smoke output on the board console path.
Toggle board LED and exercise user button mapping in the simulator manifest.
Run unsupported-instruction audit against the selected smoke firmware.
open_questions: Which UART instance is routed to the on-board ST-LINK virtual COM port?
Which exact LED GPIO pins and active levels are used on this board revision?
Is BLE scope limited to example selection and documentation, or is CPU2/radio behavior expected in simulation?
